summary.intent: Address a fifth round of codex review comments on PR #678 (ars-ui) covering splitter collapse/expand invariants, then commit the fixes with spec updates.
summary.outcome: Three new codex threads were resolved: collapse now preserves total size when another panel exists, expansion is capped at panel max, and ARIA valuemax derives from actual current total. Spec and implementation were updated in lockstep and all regression checks passed before committing.

repo: The repo uses a codex bot review loop: post '@codex review', poll for 👀 reaction while review is in progress, then act only after the eyes reaction clears and unresolved threads are stable.
Spec files in spec/components/layout/ must be kept in sync with implementation changes; cargo xtask spec validate enforces this.

Test suite is split across unit tests (layout::splitter), spec conformance (--test spec_conformance layout), proptest state machines (--test proptest_state_machines proptest_splitter --ignored), and insta snapshots (--unreferenced=reject).
Commits require --no-gpg-sign and a Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com> trailer for review-fix patches.

code.finding: collapse_panel logic updated: must preserve total distributed size when another panel is present, not just zero out the collapsed panel.
expand_panel logic updated: expansion must be capped at panel max, and ARIA aria-valuemax should reflect the actual current total rather than a static fallback.
Spec blocks describing collapse/expand and handle range behavior were out of date relative to implementation; required manual sync after each fix pass.

workflow: Poll loop checks both reactions (codex:+1 for approval, codex:eyes for in-progress) and unresolved thread count before deciding to act, avoiding partial-output false positives.
Focused test suite is run in layers: unit+snapshot first, then spec conformance, then proptest (ignored by default), then insta unreferenced-reject, then nightly fmt check.
gh api graphql is used to resolve review threads after replying, keeping the PR thread list clean.

friction: Codex posts threads incrementally while the 👀 marker is still present, requiring the poll loop to wait for the eyes reaction to clear before acting on threads to avoid partial-output churn.
Multiple sequential review passes (at least five) were needed, each requiring a full test/commit/reply/resolve cycle.
open_items: Spec conformance for collapse/expand edge cases may need further review if codex opens additional threads in a sixth pass.
PROPTEST_CASES=256 is used for the splitter proptest; whether this coverage level is sufficient for the new invariants has not been explicitly validated.
